rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qual
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of the person most qualified and custodian of records of defendant John K. Bice Co., Inc. is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to produce a deponent. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Both plaintiffs¿ and defendant¿s requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For An Order Compelling Verifications To (1) Pltfs' Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); (2) Pltfs' Responses To Requests For Production Of Docs (Set One); (3) Pltfs' Responses To Requests For Admission (Set One); And (4) Pltfs' Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One); Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, September 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rapid American Corporation¿s motion to compel verifications is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
